title: Add Stage 2 regression test suite for canonical Formam flows
goal: Lock the canonical Stage 2 baseline with automated tests covering Formam storage invariants and the first file-backed Formam command flows.
context: Wave C is the final closeout wave for Stage 2 and is intentionally limited to verification and documentation alignment.

The Stage 2 checklist explicitly calls for regression tests covering deliverable normalization, deliverable markdown round-trip, next deliverable id generation, exact parent-goal validation, goal-map markdown round-trip, and the first Formam command flows.
Wave A and Wave B already establish the canonical Formam storage model and the first Formam CLI loop, so Wave C should protect that baseline rather than broadening architecture.
Formam must remain the stage that defines deliverables and maps before any Agenda work begins.
requirements: Add automated tests for deliverable normalization into the canonical Stage 2 deliverable schema.
Add automated tests for canonical deliverable markdown round-trip behavior.
Add automated tests for next_deliverable_id() based on existing DELIV_###.md files.
Add automated tests for exact canonical parent-goal validation against the Finis storage layer.
Add automated tests for canonical goal-map markdown round-trip behavior.
Add automated tests for azq form build writing one canonical deliverable and one canonical goal map for a valid parent goal.
Add automated tests for azq form list and azq form show against canonical Formam storage.
Add automated tests for azq form map writing or refreshing a canonical goal map.
Keep the tests focused on structure invariants and CLI-visible behavior rather than speculative planning features.
acceptance_criteria: The repository has an automated regression suite for the highest-risk Stage 2 Formam storage and command flows.
The tests prove the canonical file-backed Formam model behaves deterministically.
Stage 2 behavior is locked by tests rather than manual inspection alone.
suggested_commit_message: add stage 2 regression tests for canonical formam flows

title: Align Stage 2 documentation with live Formam behavior
goal: Make the operator-facing docs teach the live Stage 2 truth by describing canonical deliverables and goal maps as active Formam artifacts and by showing that deliverables come before tasks.
context: Wave C closeout explicitly requires the docs to stop presenting Formam as doctrine only once the first live Formam command loop exists.
The Stage 2 checklist says the docs must define data/form/deliverables/ and data/form/maps/ as active Formam storage and keep the language aligned with deliverables-before-tasks.
The current README and bootstrap docs still describe Formam as mostly specified rather than implemented.
This closeout wave exists to freeze Stage 2 before Agenda begins, so documentation drift must be removed now.
requirements: Refactor AZQ_BOOTSTRAP.md so it no longer presents Formam only as a future engine once Stage 2 is complete.
Refactor README.md so the project overview reflects the live canonical Formam model.
Explicitly define data/form/deliverables/ as the canonical home for active deliverable artifacts.
Explicitly define data/form/maps/ as the canonical home for active goal-map artifacts.
Document the first live Formam command set: azq form build, azq form list, azq form show, and azq form map.
Clarify that deliverables and maps are structure artifacts and that tasks still begin later in Agenda.
Align examples and terminology around DELIV_### and GOAL_<goal_id>_MAP.md artifacts where useful.
acceptance_criteria: The docs no longer teach a pre-Stage-2 picture where Formam exists only as planned doctrine.
A new operator can read the docs and understand where canonical deliverables and goal maps live on disk.
The docs clearly preserve the craft order by teaching deliverables before tasks.
suggested_commit_message: align stage 2 docs with live formam artifacts
